name: Luke AFB
profile: Military-friendly Luke AFB housing snapshot anchored to Glendale, Litchfield Park, Goodyear, Surprise, Peoria, Avondale, Buckeye, and the Phoenix West Valley, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Large Phoenix West Valley fighter-training market with strong inventory depth, heat and utility exposure, HOA/new-build variation, and commute sensitivity by corridor
market_metrics.method: Aggregated from selected PCS housing-market ZIPs

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on May 2026 Phoenix West Valley VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Luke AFB buyers should model mortgage, property tax, homeowners insurance, HOA, utilities, summer cooling, solar lease terms, commute, property condition, and assignment length together.
Arizona property taxes may be lower than many states, but summer electricity, HOA, pool maintenance, new-build community costs, and commute exposure can materially affect true affordability.
Glendale, Litchfield Park, Goodyear, Surprise, Peoria, Avondale, Buckeye, and Verrado can each produce very different commute, school, HOA, and resale outcomes.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan estimate for Glendale / Luke AFB / Phoenix West Valley area

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military access with controlled entry and 24/7 base security.
crime_status.metro_glendale_west_valley: Glendale, Goodyear, Surprise, Peoria, Avondale, Buckeye, and the Phoenix West Valley vary by exact neighborhood, school district, HOA, traffic corridor, property condition, and resale profile. Military families should evaluate exact address, commute route, school assignment, safety, HOA, utilities, and long-term affordability before committing.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 school system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Dysart Unified, Litchfield Elementary, Agua Fria Union, Peoria Unified, Glendale Union, Deer Valley, and surrounding districts vary by exact address. Families should verify school assignment, commute, and support services before choosing housing.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by Luke AFB, Phoenix West Valley growth, new construction, healthcare, logistics, sports/entertainment, retirees, and broader Phoenix metro demand.

rental_metrics.notes: Planning estimate for 3-bedroom military-family rentals across Glendale, Litchfield Park, Goodyear, Surprise, Peoria, Avondale, and nearby Luke AFB commute corridors.
rental_metrics.method: Aggregated from selected PCS housing-market ZIPs

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Major F-35 and fighter-training base with strong West Valley growth, broad housing inventory, robust spouse employment access, Phoenix metro amenities, and multiple family-friendly suburban corridors.

financial_brief.bah_vs_buy_position: Favorable with heat, HOA, and commute discipline
financial_brief.affordability_summary: Luke AFB offers broad Phoenix West Valley housing choice, but higher home prices, summer utilities, HOA fees, pool/solar obligations, and commute congestion can tighten the true monthly picture.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers targeting clean, well-located homes in Glendale, Litchfield Park, Goodyear, Surprise, Peoria, Avondale, or Buckeye with practical commute, strong condition, clear HOA terms, and residual income room.

market_bluf.bluf_headline: Phoenix West Valley Fighter Training Market
market_bluf.bluf_summary: Luke AFB gives military families broad West Valley housing choice and strong lifestyle access, but the best housing move depends on commute corridor, summer cooling cost, HOA/new-build fees, school zone, property condition, and assignment length.

summary_points: Luke AFB sits in the Phoenix West Valley, one of the largest and fastest-growing suburban military-adjacent markets in the Air Force portfolio.

Glendale and Litchfield Park provide the closest practical base access, while Goodyear, Surprise, Peoria, Avondale, Buckeye, and Verrado create different school, price, lifestyle, and commute tradeoffs.
Home prices are materially higher than many central or southern military markets, but broad inventory and new-build supply can create buyer choice.
Arizona property taxes may be manageable, but summer electric bills, HOA, solar leases, pool maintenance, and commute time can change the real monthly answer.
The best Luke AFB housing decision balances BAH, commute, school zones, HOA, utilities, home condition, heat exposure, and assignment length.
opportunities: Broad Phoenix West Valley inventory gives buyers and renters more options than smaller military towns.
Strong defense, aviation, healthcare, logistics, sports, tourism, and Phoenix metro employment support housing relevance.
Litchfield Park, Goodyear, Surprise, Peoria, and Verrado can offer strong family lifestyle options for households with payment room.
VA buyers may find durable value if they control HOA, utilities, solar obligations, and commute exposure.
Renters can use the size of the metro to compare school zones, commute routes, and lease flexibility before buying.
risks: Summer cooling costs can materially affect the household budget.
HOA fees, solar leases, pool maintenance, and new-build community fees should be reviewed carefully.
Commute from Buckeye, far Surprise, far Peoria, or parts of Phoenix can become frustrating.
School zones and neighborhood fit vary significantly by exact address.
Short assignments may favor renting if transaction costs, resale timing, or market softness are concerns.
buyer_notes: Luke AFB buyers should compare Glendale, Litchfield Park, Goodyear, Surprise, Peoria, Avondale, Buckeye, and Verrado by commute, school zone, HOA, utilities, property condition, and resale logic.
Do not judge affordability by purchase price alone; summer electricity, HOA, solar lease terms, pool cost, and commute time must be included.
A slightly higher-priced home with better insulation, newer HVAC, lower HOA, and shorter commute may be better than a cheaper home with high utility and driving cost.
New-build incentives can be attractive, but buyers should review tax estimates, HOA, community fees, lot location, builder warranty, and resale competition.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who preserve residual income and avoid stretching into a payment that only works before utilities and HOA.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around Luke commute, VA readiness, HVAC condition, low utility features, HOA clarity, school-zone appeal, and practical monthly value.
Move-in-ready homes with newer HVAC, roof, windows, insulation, shade, solar clarity, and PCS-friendly timing can stand out.
Military buyers respond well to realistic payment logic and clear maintenance history.
Homes in strong Luke commute corridors should emphasize base access, family practicality, utility efficiency, and resale usability.
investor_angles: Military demand, Phoenix growth, healthcare, logistics, sports/entertainment, and retiree migration support rental relevance across the West Valley.
Cash flow depends heavily on acquisition price, HOA, taxes, insurance, maintenance, pool cost, solar obligations, and vacancy.
Investors should stress-test summer utility expectations, HVAC replacement, tenant turnover, HOA restrictions, and new-build competition.
Clean single-family homes in practical Luke commute corridors may outperform cheaper properties with weak schools, high HOA, or poor utility profile.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in payment, not just list price, as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, HOA, solar terms, utilities, commute, property condition, school assignment, and resale quality before locking a neighborhood.
This market rewards disciplined buyers more than emotional stretch buyers.
A lower purchase price can be helpful, but only if the home has realistic commute, utility efficiency, HOA terms, and resale appeal.
seller_guidance: Price against current inventory and days on market, not older peak assumptions.
Condition and presentation matter more when buyers have broader Phoenix-area options.
Military-adjacent demand can be a strength if framed correctly with commute, condition, utility efficiency, and monthly value.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near Luke AFB and West Valley commute corridors.
Investors should still model vacancy, HOA, tax drag, insurance, HVAC, pool service, solar obligations, and maintenance conservatively.
A good rental here is about stable spread, clean condition, practical base access, and manageable summer utility profile — not just headline rent.

market.market_type_summary: Phoenix West Valley military market with broad inventory, fast-moving well-priced homes, heat/utility overlays, and HOA-sensitive affordability

base_profile.market_label: Glendale / Phoenix West Valley / Luke Military Housing Market
base_profile.installation_type: Fighter Training / F-35 / Air Education and Training Command Base
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 56th Fighter Wing / Air Education and Training Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Luke AFB is a major Air Education and Training Command fighter-training installation near Glendale, Arizona. The base supports F-35 and fighter pilot training, fighter readiness, instructor cadre, student throughput, maintenance, and mission support functions tied closely to the Phoenix West Valley.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Fighter-training-focused, Phoenix-metro-connected, hot-desert, HOA-sensitive, commute-sensitive, family-relevant, inventory-rich, and highly tied to Glendale, Litchfield Park, Goodyear, Surprise, Peoria, Avondale, Buckeye, and Verrado housing choices.
base_profile.base_bluf: Luke AFB is not just a Glendale housing decision. It is a BAH, summer-utility, HOA, commute-route, school-zone, solar-lease, new-build, and assignment-length decision. The Phoenix West Valley gives families options, but the wrong commute or monthly utility profile can quietly break the budget.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize assignment length, school-zone verification, BAH fit, HOA review, summer cooling cost, commute route, property condition, and resale logic before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, roommate/rental flexibility, daily services, gym access, and avoiding unnecessary overbuying in lifestyle corridors.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Luke AFB housing conversations are about BAH realism, West Valley commute corridors, HOA/solar clarity, HVAC and utility cost, school-zone confidence, and Phoenix-market resale strategy.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Luke is influenced by fighter training, instructor assignments, Phoenix West Valley growth, healthcare, logistics, sports/entertainment, retirees, and demand for clean single-family rentals in practical commute corridors.

visitor_control_center.name: Luke AFB Visitor Reception Center
visitor_control_center.address: Verify current Lightning Gate visitor routing with official Luke AFB sources before arrival
visitor_control_center.phone: Verify current phone number with official Luke AFB Security Forces sources
visitor_control_center.hours: Monday-Friday, 0800-1600; closed weekends, holidays, and base down days; passes may be processed outside VRC/CRC hours by installation entry controllers at South Gate when authorized
best_for: Family members without routine installation access
visitor_control_center.note: Use official Luke AFB installation access procedures before arrival. Access requirements, sponsor rules, gate procedures, VRC/CRC hours, and pass rules can change.

gates.name: South Gate
gates.hours: 24/7 per public gate references; verify current official status before travel
gates.commute_notes: Primary 24/7 gate assumption for PCS planning and after-hours access. Useful anchor point when users are uncertain about gate availability.
gates.user_warning: Verify current gate status with official Luke AFB sources before travel.

gates.name: Lightning Gate
gates.hours: Public references commonly list Lightning Gate as daily limited access; official VRC hours are Monday-Friday 0800-1600. Verify current gate and VRC hours before travel.
gates.commute_notes: Important for visitor and daytime access planning, but users should confirm operating hours before relying on it as the primary commute gate.
gates.user_warning: Do not assume VRC hours and gate drive-through hours are identical. Confirm official gate status before routing users.

gates.name: North Gate
gates.hours: Public references commonly list North Gate as weekday limited commuter access; verify current official status before travel
gates.commute_notes: May help some commute patterns depending on exact residence, report time, and current access rules.
gates.user_warning: Limited gate hours can affect commute reliability. Do not assume availability outside posted windows.

gates.name: Commercial / Large Vehicle Access
gates.hours: Verify official Luke AFB commercial access procedures
gates.commute_notes: Useful for commercial or delivery planning, not a default family commute gate.
gates.user_warning: Movers and commercial deliveries should verify procedures through official base access guidance.

commute_intelligence.commute_bluf: For Luke AFB, commute risk is mostly corridor-based. Glendale and Litchfield Park can be very practical, while far Surprise, Peoria, Buckeye, and Verrado require honest drive testing. Gate availability, report time, heat, freeway congestion, and school traffic all matter.
primary_commute_factors: Lightning Gate visitor and daily access rules
North Gate weekday or limited commuter access
Loop 101, I-10, Loop 303, Northern Avenue, Camelback Road, and Glendale Avenue congestion
School traffic and major event traffic
Exact duty location on or near the installation
Shift schedule or early report time

commute_bands.label: Close-in
commute_bands.fit: Best for users prioritizing proximity and daily convenience
commute_bands.watchout: Close-in areas still need property, school, HOA, and condition screening.

commute_bands.label: Balanced
commute_bands.fit: Often the best balance between housing quality, schools, services, and commute
commute_bands.watchout: Gate choice, school traffic, and exact home condition can still change the real answer.

commute_bands.label: Lifestyle tradeoff
commute_bands.fit: May work for users wanting stronger schools, newer homes, or broader West Valley amenities
commute_bands.watchout: Can become inconvenient for early reporting, frequent base errands, or family needs.

commute_bands.label: Extended commute
commute_bands.fit: Only for users with strong household reasons to live farther from base
commute_bands.watchout: Longer commute can overpower newer-home appeal or lifestyle preference.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Use South Gate as the primary 24/7 gate assumption.
Check Lightning Gate as a visitor and possible commute helper only when the user's route and schedule fit current operating rules.
Check North Gate only as a limited commuter helper when current official hours support it.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on newness, school reputation, or builder incentives.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, users should test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window they would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Luke-area housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, HOA, summer utilities, solar lease terms, property condition, debt, and assignment length. BAH can support buying for some households, but Phoenix West Valley prices, HOA, and cooling costs can make renting smarter for short timelines or tight residual income.
recommended_calculations: BAH with dependents vs estimated rent
BAH without dependents vs estimated rent
Total housing cost vs 30% income lane
Residual monthly income after expenses and housing
HOA, solar, utility, and assignment-length sensitivity
bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter, not a promise of affordability. The Base tab should push users into the PCSUnited financial calculator when the housing gap, summer utilities, HOA, commute, or assignment length looks tight.

family_readiness.family_bluf: Families PCSing to Luke AFB should verify school zones, childcare options, medical access, spouse employment, commute, summer utility costs, HOA rules, and housing availability before choosing a neighborhood.

school_guidance.school_zone_warning: Always verify school assignment by exact address before signing a lease or purchase contract.
school_guidance.nearby_school_district_note: Common Luke-area school research may include Dysart Unified, Litchfield Elementary, Agua Fria Union, Peoria Unified, Glendale Union, Deer Valley, and other address-specific districts across the Phoenix West Valley.
school_guidance.school_liaison_note: Use official school liaison resources where available for military school transition support.

childcare_guidance.cdc_note: Child Development Center availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified directly with official base resources.
childcare_guidance.youth_program_note: Youth programs and school-age care can materially affect family housing decisions.

medical_guidance.medical_warning: Verify clinic availability, pharmacy procedures, and emergency care options directly with official medical sources before relying on any single facility.

family_readiness.spouse_employment_note: The Phoenix metro offers broad spouse employment across healthcare, education, logistics, aviation, technology, retail, sports/entertainment, and government-adjacent sectors, but commute and childcare should be considered together.

before_arrival: Verify official gate and Visitor Reception Center hours.
Confirm sponsor contact and unit reporting instructions.
Confirm lodging or temporary housing plan.
Review housing office guidance before signing any lease or purchase contract.
Compare BAH against rent, mortgage, HOA, taxes, insurance, utilities, and debt.
Shortlist neighborhoods by likely gate and commute band.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Check pet policies for on-base housing, rentals, and temporary lodging.
Review HOA rules, solar lease terms, pool maintenance, and summer utility expectations.
first_week: Visit or contact the housing office if still deciding between on-base and off-base options.
Drive the commute from target neighborhoods during expected report time.
Tour properties before committing when possible.
Avoid choosing purely by new-build incentives, low rent, or lifestyle photos.
first_30_days: Finalize housing only after comparing commute, BAH fit, school zones, HOA, property condition, utility cost, resale risk, heat exposure, and total monthly cost.
Save emergency, medical, gate, and housing office contact information.
Update PCSUnited profile and financial readiness dashboard with final housing numbers.
Review whether the selected area supports the user's assignment length and family needs.

home_price.provider: Zillow / Realtor.com / Glendale and Phoenix West Valley housing market observations

parser_notes: This Luke AFB file follows the same tempo and structure as the uploaded Lackland.json reference, including market metrics, costs, ownership notes, rental metrics, bedroom bands, housing market object, base_profile, gates, major services, on-base housing, recommended neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, family readiness, PCS watchouts, realtor intelligence, data confidence, source notes, and compatibility notes.
Base-facing label is set to Luke AFB, while Glendale and Phoenix West Valley are preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.
This file includes the shared placeholder base_image_url requested for bases without finished map images.
Gate and housing office details are aligned to official Luke AFB, Air Force Housing, Military OneSource, and public base-directory sources as of June 27, 2026.

market_area.method: Uses selected PCS buyer/renter ZIPs, not installation-only ZIP.

demographics.method: Place-level ACS profile where geo_id is available

nearby_services.method: Preserved from existing PCSU base JSON

notes: The JSON structure is preserved from the existing PCSU base file.
RentCast updates housing, sale, and rental market fields.
Census updates demographic and economic fields only when values are valid.
Curated PCS guidance, official links, gates, major services, and base_profile intelligence are preserved unless manually updated.
